references.text: Nooteboom, HP. 1962. Simaroubaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 6: 196-198

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 284 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 345; Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 196.
species.distribution: Africa, America, Andaman Isls, Australia, Caribbean, India, Indian Ocean, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Moluccas, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam. BURIAS, CUYO ARCHIPELAGO, LUMBUCAN, PAMALICAN.
species.notes: Coastal shrub of the Barringtonia formation, usually rare, but sometimes locally forming thickets along the sandy beach and along coral coasts. Isolated oceanic islands and islets, largely avoiding continents. Fl. & fr. throughout the year. 0m.
